params.summary: Sprint 1 — Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.
params.description: Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.

params.summary: Task Board
params.epic_link: Sprint 1 — Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.
params.acceptance_criteria:
Users can create, update, and delete tasks with all relevant fields.
Tasks can be assigned to existing team members.
Due dates and priority levels can be set and updated on tasks.
Tasks can be moved between 'To Do', 'In Progress', and 'Done' columns via drag-and-drop.
Task board updates are reflected in real-time for all team members.

params.summary: Design task data model including assignment, due date, priority, and status
Implement CRUD endpoints for tasks with validation and permissions
Implement drag-and-drop functionality on frontend with state sync
Implement real-time update mechanism for task board (e.g. Websockets)
Frontend UI for task creation, editing, deletion, and assignment
Backend logic to handle assignment and update notifications
QA: POST /tasks - create task with valid fields: title, description, assigned_to (existing member), due_date, priority; verify task appears in 'To Do' column
QA: PUT /tasks/{task_id} - update task fields individually and in combination (e.g. change assigned_to, due_date, priority) and verify updates persist
QA: DELETE /tasks/{task_id} - delete existing task and verify removal from task board for all users
QA: PATCH /tasks/{task_id}/move - drag-and-drop task between 'To Do', 'In Progress', and 'Done', verify backend updates and real-time sync
QA: Assign task to non-existent team member and verify error handling
QA: Attempt task creation with missing required fields and validate error responses
QA: Verify real-time task updates propagate to multiple users logged in concurrently
QA: Verify editing and moving tasks respects user permissions (e.g. only assigned or authorized users can edit/move)
QA: Verify setting due_date to past date is handled according to spec (reject or accept)
QA: Validate task priority accepts only allowed values and rejects invalid priorities
User Authentication
params.epic_link: Sprint 1 — Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.
params.acceptance_criteria:
Users can sign up and log in with valid email and password.
JWT tokens are issued upon login and validated on protected endpoints.
Users can request a password reset email and successfully reset their password.
Invalid login attempts are handled securely with proper error messages.

params.summary: Design and implement user schema with secure password storage
Implement signup and login endpoints with validation and error handling
Implement JWT token issuance, validation, and middleware for protected routes
Integrate password reset email generation and verification endpoints
Set up email service for sending reset emails
Create frontend forms for signup, login, and password reset
QA: POST /signup with valid email and password to verify account creation succeeds
QA: POST /signup with already registered email to verify duplicate registration rejection
QA: POST /signup with invalid email format to verify input validation
QA: POST /signup with weak password (e.g., less than 8 chars or no complexity) to verify policy enforcement
QA: POST /login with correct email/password returns valid JWT token in response body
QA: POST /login with incorrect password returns proper error message without revealing if email exists
QA: Access protected endpoint GET /user/profile with valid JWT token returns 200 and user info
QA: Access protected endpoint with expired JWT token returns 401 Unauthorized
QA: Access protected endpoint with malformed JWT token returns 401 Unauthorized
QA: POST /password-reset-request with valid email triggers password reset email
QA: POST /password-reset-request with unregistered email returns success response without leaking existence
QA: POST /password-reset with valid token and new password updates password and allows login
QA: POST /password-reset with expired or invalid token fails securely with proper error
QA: Verify stored passwords are hashed with salt and not stored in plaintext in database
Team Management
params.epic_link: Sprint 1 — Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.
params.acceptance_criteria:
Users can create teams and send email invitations to join.
Invited members receive invitations and can accept them.
Role-based access control is enforced across the app based on assigned roles.
Users can see their role in the team interface.

params.summary: Create team and membership data models with roles
Implement endpoints for team creation and invitation sending
Set up email invitation workflow and acceptance endpoints
Implement role assignment and access control middleware
Frontend UI for team creation, member listing, role display, and invite management
QA: POST /teams with valid payload creates a new team and returns correct team_id
QA: POST /teams/{team_id}/invite with valid email sends invitation email
QA: Verify email content includes unique invitation token and correct team info
QA: GET /invitations/{token} returns invitation details and expiration status
QA: POST /invitations/{token}/accept associates user with team and assigns correct role
QA: Verify role-based access restrictions on team resources for Admin, Member, and Viewer roles
QA: Check UI displays current user's role in team management interface
QA: Verify updating a user's role via PATCH /teams/{team_id}/members/{user_id} enforces permissions
QA: Ensure users without Admin role cannot invite or change roles
QA: Verify email invitation tokens expire or are revocable (once expired or revoked invitation cannot be accepted)
QA: Test user belonging to multiple teams and verify role scoping per team
QA: Verify audit logs track role changes and invitations sent
Notifications
params.epic_link: Sprint 1 — Deliver core high-priority features User Authentication, Task Board, and Team Management with thorough QA focus despite high risks.
params.acceptance_criteria:
Email notifications are sent promptly when a task assignment occurs.
In-app notifications appear for tasks nearing their due date based on configurable timeframes.
Users can view and dismiss in-app notifications.
Notification delivery failures are logged and monitored.

params.summary: Implement backend triggers/events for task assignment and approaching due dates
Integrate email sending routines for task assignments
Design and implement in-app notification storage and retrieval APIs
Create frontend components for displaying and dismissing notifications
Implement logging and monitoring for notification delivery status
QA: Verify POST /notifications/email triggers an email immediately upon task assignment with correct recipient and content.
QA: Check GET /notifications/in-app returns all pending notifications accurately filtered by user ID and due date proximity.
QA: Test dismissing an in-app notification updates its status via PUT /notifications/in-app/:notificationId/dismiss and it no longer appears in subsequent fetches.
QA: Validate configurable timeframes for due date reminders correctly filter which tasks generate in-app notifications.
QA: Simulate email delivery failure and verify proper error logging in notification delivery logs with timestamp and task ID.
QA: Confirm that multiple reassignments of the same task send an email notification each time to the new assignee only.
QA: Ensure in-app notifications are persistent and reloaded correctly after user logs out and back in.
QA: Check that dismissed notifications do not reappear unless the due date or assignment changes.
QA: Verify that simultaneous changes (due date change and reassignment) trigger both notification types correctly.
Reporting
params.description: Excluded due to sprint capacity constraints and lower priority compared to high priority features.
